english: Damn Good Kaya and Salty Butter

serving.description: 1 serving (1 grilled soft bun with kaya and butter)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one grilled handmade soft bun spread generously with kaya custard and salty butter, weighing approximately 120 g.

nutrient_source.title: Kaya Toast — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA SR Legacy composite estimate for bread, kaya spread, and butter components
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB Foodo entry for 'Sinpopo Damn Good Kaya and Salty Butter' exists. Nutrient values are estimated from a composite of: (1) HPB Foodo entry for 'Kaya Toast' (soft white bread bun ~60 g, kaya spread ~25 g, butter ~10 g), and (2) USDA SR Legacy entries for white bread (NDB 18069), kaya/coconut egg jam (analogous to coconut cream-based spread), and salted butter (NDB 01001). Per-100g values are derived from the weighted composite of these three components at approximate proportions: bread 50%, kaya 21%, butter 8%, with the remainder being moisture loss from grilling. Values are rounded to one decimal place.

gi_source.title: White bread GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for kaya toast as a composite dish. GI is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component: white bread/soft white bun, which has a well-established GI of approximately 70 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283). Kaya spread (sugar and coconut milk base) and butter contribute additional sugar and fat respectively; fat content moderates glycemic response slightly, but the high-GI white bread base dominates. GI estimated at 70 (high). Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (70 × 42.5 × 1.20) / 100 = 35.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g; ~372 mg per serving) from salted butter and bread
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit kaya toast consumption to occasional servings and request unsalted butter where available, as the salted butter and bread contribute a meaningful sodium load (~372 mg per 120 g serving, approximately 16% of the 2300 mg daily limit).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) and significant sugar content (~14.8 g/100 g) from kaya spread and white bread base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat kaya toast as an occasional indulgence only, given its high GI (~70) and high glycemic load (~35.7 per serving), which can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with a protein-rich beverage such as kopi-o kosong may partially blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~8.1 g/100 g) and cholesterol (~55 mg/100 g) from butter and egg-based kaya
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit kaya toast frequency due to its saturated fat content (~8.1 g/100 g, approximately 9.7 g per serving), which exceeds one-third of the recommended daily saturated fat limit of 20 g, and its moderate cholesterol contribution from the egg-based kaya custard.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kaya and Butter Toast (also known as Kaya Toast or Roti Bakar Kaya) is a quintessential Singaporean breakfast and snack dish consisting of a grilled or toasted soft white bread bun or sliced bread generously spread with kaya — a rich, fragrant coconut and egg custard jam sweetened with sugar and flavoured with pandan leaves — and a thick slab of cold salted butter. The Sinpopo Coffee version, branded 'Damn Good Kaya and Salty Butter', uses a handmade soft bun that is grilled to achieve a lightly crisp exterior while remaining pillowy inside, elevating the traditional hawker-centre staple into a café-quality experience. Kaya toast is traditionally served as part of a Singaporean breakfast set alongside soft-boiled eggs seasoned with soy sauce and white pepper, and a cup of kopi (local coffee) or teh (local tea). The flavour profile is sweet, buttery, and subtly coconut-fragrant, with the cold salty butter providing a savoury contrast to the sweet kaya. Nutritionally, kaya toast is energy-dense (~318 kcal/100 g), with a significant carbohydrate load (~42.5 g/100 g) dominated by refined white bread and sugar-rich kaya, a moderate fat content (~14.2 g/100 g) largely from saturated fats in butter and coconut milk, and a moderate sodium content (~310 mg/100 g) from the salted butter and bread. Its high glycemic index (~70) makes it a high-glycemic food that can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation, making it a food of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. It is suitable for vegetarians and is generally considered halal. The dish is a cultural icon of Singapore's kopitiam (coffee shop) heritage and is widely available across hawker centres, kopitiams, and specialty cafés island-wide.
common_user_queries: How many calories in kaya toast?
What is the GI of kaya toast?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific Sinpopo branded product. Nutrient values are estimated from a composite of HPB Foodo kaya toast reference data and USDA SR Legacy component data for white bread, kaya-type spread, and salted butter. GI is estimated from the white bread dominant component using the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level data is well-supported and the dish composition is well-understood.
notes: 1. This record represents the Sinpopo Coffee 'Damn Good Kaya and Salty Butter' product (GD003049), a premium café version of the classic Singaporean kaya toast. The canonical food name has been normalised to 'Kaya and Butter Toast' for database consistency; the branded name is preserved in aliases. 2. Serving size of 120 g is estimated based on a typical handmade soft bun (~60 g) plus kaya spread (~25 g) and butter (~10 g) with grilling moisture loss; actual Sinpopo product weight may differ. 3. Nutrient values are composite estimates; exact values for this specific product would require laboratory analysis or direct disclosure from Sinpopo Coffee. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are not directly reported by HPB Foodo for this specific product. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical kaya toast ingredients (no pork, no alcohol); consumers should verify with Sinpopo Coffee directly. 6. Price SGD 8.00 per serving reflects the premium café positioning of this product relative to hawker-centre kaya toast (~SGD 1.20–2.00).
